title: Charmed Life - Chapter 17 (Fragment 1)
text:
Chapter 14

14

C
 AT SCREAMED . Flames burst out of him all over. He screamed again, and beat at himself with flaming hands, and went on screaming. They were pale, shimmering, transparent flames. They burst out through his clothes, and his shoes, his hair, across his face, so that, in seconds, he was wrapped in pale flame from head to foot. He fell on the floor, still screaming, and rolled there, blazing.

Janet kept her presence of mind. She dragged up the nearest corner of the carpet and threw it over Cat. She had heard that this smothered flames. But it did not smother these. To Janet’s horror, the pale, ghostly flames came straight through the carpet as if it was not there, and played on the black underside of it more fiercely than ever. They did not burn the carpet, nor did they burn Janet’s hands as she frantically rolled Cat over in the carpet, and then over again. But no matter how much carpet she wrapped around Cat, the flames still came through, and Cat went on blazing and screaming. His head was half outside the flaming bundle she had made of him, and it was a sheaf of flames. She could see his screaming face inside the fire.

Janet did the only other thing she could think of. She jumped up and screamed herself. “Chrestomanci, Chrestomanci! Come quickly !”

The door burst open while she was still screaming. Janet had forgotten it was locked, but the lock did not bother Chrestomanci. She could see it sticking out from the edge of the door as he flung it open. She had forgotten there were guests to dinner too. She remembered when she saw Chrestomanci’s lace ruffles, and his black velvet suit which glimmered all over like an opal, blue, crimson, yellow, and green. But that did not seem to bother Chrestomanci either. He took one look at the flaming bundle on the floor and said “Good God!” Then he was down on his elegant knees unwrapping the carpet as frantically as Janet had wrapped it.

“I’m awfully sorry. I thought that would help,” Janet stammered.

“It ought to have done,” said Chrestomanci, rolling Cat over, with flames whirling through, over, and along his velvet arms. “How did he do it?”

“He struck one of the matches. I told him—”

“You stupid child!” Chrestomanci was so angry that Janet burst into tears. He lugged at the last of the carpet and Cat rolled free, flaming like a straw faggot. He was not really screaming anymore. He was making a long thin noise that had Janet covering her ears. Chrestomanci dived into the heart of the flames and found the book of matches. It was tightly clasped in Cat’s right hand. “Thank God he didn’t have it in the left one,” he said. “Go and turn your shower on. Quick!”

“Of course. Of course,” Janet sobbed, and raced to do it.

She fumbled with the taps and had just got a strong spray of cold water hissing into the sunken blue bath as Chrestomanci hurried in carrying Cat, in a ball of roaring flame. He dumped Cat down into the bath and held him there, turning him this way and that to get him wet all over.

Cat steamed and hissed. The water coming from the sprayhead shone like water against the sun, golden as the sun itself. It came down like a beam of light. And, as the bath began to fill up, Cat seemed to be turning and threshing in a pool of sunshine. He boiled it into golden bubbles. The room filled with steam. Coils of smoke drifted up from the bath, smelling thick and sweet. It was the same smell that Janet remembered from the morning she had first found herself there. As far as she could see through the smoke, Cat seemed to be turning black in the golden pool. But the water was wet. Chrestomanci was getting soaked.

vocabulary.definition: a collection of things fastened or wrapped together
vocabulary.example: The flaming bundle on the floor.

vocabulary.definition: grasped or held tightly with the hand
vocabulary.example: Clasped in Cat’s right hand.

vocabulary.definition: spiral or twisted shapes or formations
vocabulary.example: Coils of smoke.

vocabulary.definition: moved slowly and smoothly
vocabulary.example: Smoke drifted up.

vocabulary.definition: pleasingly graceful and stylish in appearance or manner
vocabulary.example: He was down on his elegant knees.

vocabulary.definition: in a powerful or violent manner
vocabulary.example: The flames played on the black underside of the carpet more fiercely than ever.

vocabulary.definition: the hot, bright, glowing gases that come from fire
vocabulary.example: He was wrapped in pale flame from head to foot.

vocabulary.definition: in a hurried, excited, or disorganized manner due to urgency or fear
vocabulary.example: Janet frantically rolled Cat over in the carpet.

vocabulary.definition: to do something awkwardly, especially when using the hands
vocabulary.example: She fumbled with the taps.

vocabulary.definition: like a ghost in appearance or atmosphere; pale and eerie
vocabulary.example: The pale, ghostly flames came straight through the carpet.

vocabulary.definition: shone faintly or unsteadily; gleamed softly
vocabulary.example: His black velvet suit glimmered all over.

vocabulary.definition: decorative frills or gathers made of lace fabric, often on clothing
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci’s lace ruffles.

vocabulary.definition: to pull or carry something heavy or cumbersome with effort
vocabulary.example: He lugged at the last of the carpet.

vocabulary.definition: the ability to think clearly and act quickly in an emergency
vocabulary.example: Janet kept her presence of mind.

vocabulary.definition: making a very loud, deep sound
vocabulary.example: Ball of roaring flame.

vocabulary.definition: to make a loud, high-pitched cry or noise, especially because of fear, pain, or excitement
vocabulary.example: Cat screamed as flames burst out of him.

vocabulary.definition: shining with a soft tremulous or flickering light
vocabulary.example: They were pale, shimmering, transparent flames.

vocabulary.definition: to cover something completely to stop it from burning or to suffocate
vocabulary.example: She threw the carpet over Cat to smother the flames.

vocabulary.definition: crying noisily while catching one's breath
vocabulary.example: Janet sobbed.

vocabulary.definition: the part of a shower or hose that sprays water
vocabulary.example: Sprayhead producing water.

vocabulary.definition: to speak with involuntary pauses and repetitions
vocabulary.example: Janet stammered, “I’m awfully sorry.”

vocabulary.definition: emitted steam; gave off vapour
vocabulary.example: Cat steamed and hissed.

vocabulary.definition: to move or strike violently
vocabulary.example: Cat was threshing in the pool.

vocabulary.definition: allowing light to pass through so that objects behind can be distinctly seen
vocabulary.example: Transparent flames burst through his clothes.

vocabulary.definition: spinning rapidly
vocabulary.example: Flames whirling through his arms.

vocabulary.definition: covered or enclosed something completely with paper, cloth, or other material
vocabulary.example: He was wrapped in pale flame from head to foot.
